chapter.title: 组织设计
chapter.source_path: /Users/tianqiang/LifeAtlas/200_原始资料/270_电子书与书籍资料/2026-06-20_埃隆之书_中文版.epub#/OEBPS/Eric-Jorgenson_The-Book-of-Elon_PRODUCTION_v101-16.xhtml

controller_inputs.baseline_summary_trap: Do not reduce this chapter to flat communication, tolerating failure, simplicity, and a five-step method. The core issue is how organizational feedback routes let reality reach the people who can correct it before organizational defects become product, manufacturing, cost, and process defects.
controller_inputs.previous_round_hard_issue: The prior passed chapter had a P2 that the lens should concretely illuminate the text. This round must directly connect organization boundaries, communication routes, shop-floor feedback, deletion, simplification, failure tolerance, and algorithm order.
allowed_lenses: Organization as a reality feedback route, not merely a reporting structure.
Product redundancies can be physical traces of organizational boundaries.
Simplification is the ordered removal of wrong requirements, redundant parts, redundant processes, and premature automation.
Failure tolerance is useful only when bounded and converted into iteration, not when it excuses harm or recklessness.

source_anchors: Employees must be allowed to communicate directly and make the right thing happen.
The seven-step command-chain relay is criticized as deeply foolish.
Communication should follow the shortest path to the goal rather than the command chain.
Internal islands and us-versus-them thinking damage the whole company.
Box-within-box designs and the Model 3 battery-pack cover show organization boundaries in the product.
Design, engineering, and manufacturing must stay tightly coupled; line workers should be able to challenge designers immediately.
Go directly to the source of the problem, including asking welders about Starship bulkhead thickness.
Acronyms and invented terms can become a communication wall.
Innovation requires tolerance for failure, except catastrophic failure, and failure costs must be bounded.
Over-punishing failure trains people into conservative, incremental behavior.
Simplicity improves reliability and lowers cost; deleting code or parts can be more valuable than adding them.
The best part is no part; the best process is no process.
Single-piece casting reduces seams, sealants, dissimilar metals, shop size, and robots.
The algorithm order is: question requirements, delete, simplify or optimize, accelerate, automate.
Premature automation, acceleration, and optimization are errors when the target should not exist.
